headline: Robinhood requires full KYC account approval before any deposit, trade, or withdrawal; no permissionless or alternative access path exists
short_headline: Mandatory KYC wall; no open access

findings.text: Robinhood is a centralized brokerage/exchange (FINRA-registered broker-dealer, SEC-registered). All user-facing entry points — deposit, trade, withdrawal — are gated behind a mandatory account creation and identity verification (KYC/AML) process. There are no publicly accessible smart contract entry points; all asset custody is held by Robinhood. The KYC requirement constitutes a functional equivalent of a whitelist enforced at the admission layer for every user action.

All admission paths are controlled by Robinhood's centralized backend operators. Deposits, trades, and withdrawals each require an approved Robinhood account. There is no permissionless path: a single centralized operator (Robinhood Markets, Inc.) must approve each account before any user action is admitted. Robinhood can freeze, restrict, or close accounts unilaterally, as documented in its Customer Agreement.

A3-active: The official frontend (robinhood.com) enforces a hard KYC wall — users must submit government-issued ID, SSN (for US), and pass identity verification before accessing any trading or transfer functions. Additionally, Robinhood crypto services are geo-restricted and unavailable in numerous jurisdictions. The app/website actively blocks non-eligible users from transacting; this is not a passive ToS disclaimer but a runtime enforcement mechanism that prevents the UI from rendering trade/deposit/withdrawal functionality for unapproved users.

A3b-ii: No independent alternative access paths exist. Robinhood does not publish a public API for direct asset deposits or withdrawals by end users, does not expose user-accessible smart contracts for direct interaction, and has no third-party frontends that can provide equivalent access. There is no CLI, SDK, or documented direct-contract path for retail users to bypass the KYC requirement. Robinhood Crypto's whitepaper/developer resources do not offer permissionless access.

Robinhood is subject to OFAC sanctions compliance as a US-regulated financial institution. Per its Customer Agreement and Help Center, Robinhood screens accounts against OFAC lists and other government watchlists. Accounts flagged are blocked or closed. This is enforced off-chain by Robinhood's compliance systems rather than via an on-chain oracle, but the effect is equivalent to an updatable blocklist controlled by a single party (Robinhood).

Read access: Public market data (prices, charts) is available without an account on robinhood.com. Write access (deposit, trade, withdrawal, transfer): fully gated — requires a verified, approved Robinhood account. All crypto asset movements are custodial, handled by Robinhood's internal systems, not user-controlled smart contracts.

Robinhood's Customer Agreement (https://cdn.robinhood.com/assets/robinhood/legal/Robinhood%20Customer%20Agreement.pdf) and Crypto User Agreement (https://robinhood.com/us/en/support/articles/crypto-user-agreement/) establish eligibility requirements including: US residency (or eligible international markets), age of majority, identity verification, and compliance with applicable laws. Crypto services page states: 'Robinhood Crypto is only available to US customers' with a small number of exceptions. Verbatim from crypto eligibility: 'To be eligible to use Robinhood Crypto, you must...complete our identity verification process.' The ToS explicitly states Robinhood may deny, restrict, or terminate access at its discretion.

steelman.red: Robinhood enforces mandatory full KYC identity verification for every user before any deposit, trade, or withdrawal is admitted, with no permissionless or alternative access path — this is a textbook admission whitelist controlled by a single centralized operator with no on-chain governance or replacement procedure.
steelman.orange: If one argues that Robinhood's KYC is standard regulated-brokerage compliance rather than a discretionary DeFi-style whitelist, the grade could be orange: users in eligible jurisdictions who pass KYC can access services without further individual approval by a committee, and the compliance process is rule-bound rather than arbitrary.
steelman.green: Robinhood's public market data, price feeds, and educational content are accessible without any account, and within eligible jurisdictions the KYC process is largely automated and non-discretionary — one could argue admission is functionally open to all eligible users.

rationale.verdict: Choosing red because Robinhood imposes a mandatory KYC identity verification wall on every write-access function (deposit, trade, withdrawal), enforced by a single centralized operator (Robinhood Markets, Inc.) with no documented replacement procedure, no permissionless alternative access path (A3b-ii), and unilateral ability to freeze or close accounts. This satisfies the red criterion: 'admission of a core user action requires approval from a single privileged operator with no documented replacement procedure.' The orange steelman fails because even if KYC is rule-bound, it is still gated by a single operator with no on-chain governance or independent redress mechanism. The green steelman fails because read-only price data access does not constitute open access to the protocol's core functions.

evidence.shows: Robinhood Crypto User Agreement stating eligibility requirements including identity verification; confirms crypto services are restricted to eligible users who complete KYC process
Robinhood help page describing account setup and identity verification requirements before crypto trading is accessible; confirms KYC wall on crypto entry
Robinhood account eligibility page stating users must be US residents (with limited exceptions), 18+, and pass identity verification to use services
Robinhood Crypto supported coins page, confirming crypto trading is a custodial service within Robinhood's platform requiring an approved account
Robinhood Customer Agreement establishing eligibility, identity verification, and Robinhood's unilateral right to deny, restrict, or terminate access to services
Robinhood OFAC/sanctions compliance page confirming accounts are screened against government watchlists and blocked or closed if flagged
Robinhood homepage showing login/signup gate with no permissionless trading or deposit functionality visible to unauthenticated users; confirms A3-active enforcement at frontend
FINRA broker-dealer registration for Robinhood Financial LLC confirming regulated status requiring KYC/AML compliance for all customer accounts

unknowns: A3b-i: Could not verify whether Robinhood has any IPFS-pinned or mirrored frontend distributions; as a CEX this is not expected but was not explicitly confirmed absent

A6: Could not extract verbatim text from Robinhood Customer Agreement PDF due to dynamic/hosted document; cited URL and paraphrased key provisions based on publicly accessible support pages and legal pages

protocol_metadata.about: Robinhood is a US-based retail brokerage and centralized exchange offering commission-free trading in stocks, ETFs, options, and cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin, Ethereum, and others. Users hold custodial accounts — Robinhood holds assets on their behalf — and interact via a mobile app or web interface. Crypto withdrawals to self-custody wallets are supported for eligible assets. Robinhood is a FINRA-registered broker-dealer and SEC-registered investment adviser, making it a regulated financial institution rather than a decentralized protocol.
